notes.text:
# Project Release Notes: Version 0.15.0 - March 10, 2022

We are delighted to announce the release of project version 0.15.0. This version brings substantial enhancements in security, observability, and performance, as well as new features and improvements in documentation. 

## New Features

1. **Unified GTM Lane**: We've added an evidence layer for self-hosted agents in the landing module which enhances data collection and tracking.

2. **North Star Workspace Metric and Opt-in Payload Pipeline**: These have been added to the telemetry module to provide better insights into usage metrics.

3. **Scope Filter on Trust Path and Clearer JWT Rotation Prompt**: The new scope filter on trust path and clearer JWT rotation prompt in our security feature will help improve user authentication and data protection.

4. **RED Metrics, SLO Document, and Parity Matrix Entry**: These additions will enhance the observability of the system, thereby improving system monitoring and performance diagnostics.

5. **X-Request-ID Middleware and Access-Log Field**: These improvements are designed to enhance system observability.

6. **Hardened POST /v1/leads**: The POST /v1/leads endpoint has been reinforced with rate limit, validation, and logging for better security.

7. **SecurityHeaders Middleware**: We've added CSP, HSTS, frame and other SecurityHeaders middleware for improved security.

8. **Service for Marketing Landing at / and Split Registry to /app**: With this feature, the landing module now accommodates a service for marketing landing at / and split registry to /app.

## Improved Features

1. **Pool Tuning for pg/mysql, LLM Cache Eviction, and Document Environment Variables**: These have been fine-tuned for better infrastructure performance.

## Bug Fixes

1. **Escaped Apostrophe in Lead-Form Alert**: The issue of an unescaped apostrophe in the lead-form alert in the landing module has been resolved.

## Documentation

1. **Drift Across README, CLAUDE.md, and Metrics Surface**: This has been closed to ensure consistency across all documentation.

2. **Sharpened Category Framing and Differentiation Messaging**: This has been added in the landing module to provide clear differentiation between product categories.

3. **Proof Metrics and Before/After Demo Section**: These have been added in the landing module to provide users with a clear understanding of the impact and benefits of the software.

## Other Updates

1. **Zep and Letta Adapters**: These have been integrated into the harness in the providers module.

2. **In-Tree Mutation Harness Gating Internal/Trust at 0.70**: This has been included in the mutation testing to enhance test reliability.

3. **Per-Category F1 Floors**: These have been added to close the silent-regression gap in the eval testing.

4. **Cross-Transport Parity Fitness Function**: This has been added in the API testing to ensure consistency across different data transport methods.

5. **Covered mcpRunWhichTestToTrust Verdict Paths**: These have been covered in the trust testing to improve the reliability of tests.

6. **Sync Roady and Relicta Artifacts**: These artifacts have been synchronized to ensure consistency.

This update advances our commitment to providing a secure, performant, and user-friendly software system. We thank you for your continued support and look forward to your feedback on these enhancements.

## Changelog

## [0.15.0] - 2022-03-10

### Added
- Unified GTM lane — evidence layer for self-hosted agents in the landing module.
- North Star workspace metric and opt-in payload pipeline in the telemetry module.
- Scope filter on trust path, clearer JWT rotation prompt for improved security.
- RED metrics, SLO document, and parity matrix entry for enhanced observability.
- X-Request-ID middleware and access-log field for improved observability.
- Operator-readable prose rationale alongside metric string in the trust module.
- Hardened POST /v1/leads with rate limit, validation, and logging for better security.
- SecurityHeaders middleware (CSP, HSTS, frame, etc) for improved security.
- ListByTestRequirementRef port and index for which-test-to-trust in the trust module.
- Test-aware epistemic provenance and conflict resolution in the trust module.
- Service for marketing landing at / and split registry to /app in the landing module.

### Changed
- Pool tuning for pg/mysql, LLM cache eviction, and document environment variables for improved infrastructure performance.

### Fixed
- Escaped apostrophe in lead-form alert in the landing module.

### Deprecated

### Removed

### Security

### Documentation
- Closed drift across README, CLAUDE.md, and metrics surface.
- Sharpened category framing and differentiation messaging in the landing module.
- Added proof metrics and before/after demo section in the landing module.

### Other
- Zep and letta adapters wired into the harness in the providers module.
- In-tree mutation harness gating internal/trust at 0.70 in the mutation testing.
- Per-category F1 floors close the silent-regression gap in the eval testing.
- Added cross-transport parity fitness function in the API testing.
- Covered mcpRunWhichTestToTrust verdict paths in the trust testing.
- Sync roady and relicta artifacts.

commits.subject: docs: close drift across README, CLAUDE.md, and metrics surface
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
docs: close drift across README, CLAUDE.md, and metrics surface

Audit found stale counts and undocumented surfaces piling up between
the recent feature ships and the operator-facing docs:

- CLAUDE.md and `mnemos metrics` both quoted out-of-date eval-case
  counts (102 / 78). Real count across data/eval/*.yaml is 133 across
  13 suites; both updated, with a comment in main.go reminding
  whoever next adds a suite to bump the constant.
- New env vars (MNEMOS_TELEMETRY_OPTIN, MNEMOS_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT,
  MNEMOS_LLM_CACHE_MAX_BYTES, MNEMOS_DB_MAX_*) shipped over the past
  weeks but never landed in the README's environment-variable table.
  All five added with one-line descriptions plus a doc link for
  telemetry.
- README's Commands table was missing seven top-level subcommands
  that exist in main.go's dispatch (init, doctor, quality, trust,
  incident, user, agent) plus the new `metrics --workspace` flag.
  Added concise rows so `git grep` and `mnemos --help` agree with
  the rendered docs.
- Added Telemetry and Reliability sections that link to the existing
  docs/telemetry.md, SLO.md, and docs/testing/mutation.md so a
  visitor lands on the privacy posture and quality gates without
  digging.

No code logic changed beyond the eval_cases constant.

commits.subject: bench(providers): zep + letta adapters wired into the harness
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
bench(providers): zep + letta adapters wired into the harness

The benchmark scaffolding had only mem0 alongside Mnemos, so head-to-
head numbers against the Zep/Letta crowd were not even runnable, let
alone publishable. This adds Zep and Letta adapters that follow the
same Provider protocol as mem0 (reset / add / query) and fail
honestly: a `_check_alive` probe raises if the upstream service is
not reachable, instead of silently returning empty results that would
score zero and look like a Mnemos win.

Both adapters honour env (ZEP_URL/ZEP_API_KEY, LETTA_URL/LETTA_TOKEN)
so the same code targets a local docker-compose instance or a remote
deployment. docker-compose.yml grows commented stanzas for the OSS
servers; uncomment to bring them up alongside Mnemos for end-to-end
runs.

RESULTS.md now documents the cross-provider matrix, the env vars per
adapter, and the multi-provider run command. Public head-to-head
numbers land here once each backend has completed runs on the same
suite + dataset split — vendor-cited numbers are explicitly NOT
re-published as ours.

commits.subject: feat(landing): unified GTM lane — evidence layer for self-hosted agents
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(landing): unified GTM lane — evidence layer for self-hosted agents

Old landing sold "Data Sovereignty & Compliance Platform" while the
README sells "Self-hosted memory for AI apps." Two products, one
codebase — kills positioning. New hero picks the lane mem0/zep/letta
compete in: "Evidence layer for AI agents you actually own."
Compliance becomes a wedge attribute, not the headline.

What changes on the page:
- New hero copy + pill marker + tightened CTAs (Install / See it work)
- Live terminal demo: mnemos process → contradiction JSON, real output
- "vs hosted memory services" comparison table covering mem0, Zep,
  Letta — 7 capability rows with a footnote that explicit benchmark
  numbers will land alongside the adapter implementations
- Proof-points strip replaces the adjective-soup social proof:
  100% mutation kill rate on trust core, 5 backends, 99.9% SLO,
  default-off telemetry — each links to the artefact in-tree
- 3-tier pricing strip: OSS (free) / Team (waitlist) / Enterprise
  (contact)
- Lead form now feeds the Team waitlist with matching microcopy

Existing serve_test.go assertions on "Mnemos" brand still pass; no
HTTP / MCP / gRPC surface changed.

commits.subject: feat(telemetry): North Star workspace metric + opt-in payload pipeline
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(telemetry): North Star workspace metric + opt-in payload pipeline

`mnemos metrics --workspace` answers the question the existing metrics
view could not: is anyone getting value? It surfaces weekly-active
runs, evidence-backed claim count, and a workspace fingerprint that
identifies the install without leaking the path.

The same shape POSTs to MNEMOS_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT when the operator
runs --telemetry-opt-in AND sets an endpoint URL. Two independent
gates: opt-in alone, with no endpoint, sends nothing; an endpoint
alone, without opt-in, sends nothing. Default is off.

Privacy posture documented in docs/telemetry.md. Payload contains
aggregated counts and build metadata only — no claim text, run names,
file paths, source IDs, or embeddings leave the host. Install ID is
a random 16-byte hex token under the data dir, rotatable by deletion.

Tests cover install-ID stability + regeneration, opt-in round-trip,
both gates closed → no-op, both open → POST with the right shape, and
5xx response surfaces as error.

commits.subject: test(mutation): in-tree mutation harness gating internal/trust at 0.70
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
test(mutation): in-tree mutation harness gating internal/trust at 0.70

Coverage hides decision-boundary bugs: a flipped > to < in a trust
score still gets line-counted as covered. The harness in tools/mutate
walks every binary operator in the target package, swaps it via
go test -overlay, and records whether the test suite catches the
mutant. Surviving mutants are coverage holes the suite cannot tell
from correct code.

Implementation is in-tree because go-mutesting panics on Go 1.24 and
external installs aren't auto-permitted. ~250 lines of go/parser +
overlay JSON, deterministic and version-pinned with the rest of the
repo.

internal/trust now sits at 1.0000 kill rate (45/45) after the targeted
tests in mutations_test.go pin every boundary. Threshold gates at 0.70.
internal/relate (0.62) and internal/query (0.55) are advisory until
their suites tighten — ratchet plan in docs/testing/mutation.md.

commits.subject: test(eval): per-category F1 floors close the silent-regression gap
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
test(eval): per-category F1 floors close the silent-regression gap

Aggregate F1 alone hid one failure mode the audit flagged: a single
suite collapsing (e.g. causal_detection 0.95 → 0.40) while another
lifts the average past the 86% aggregate floor. The aggregate gate
passes, the regression ships.

Add per-category floors inside TestPrecisionRecall, keyed on the
first-tag category already used in the report:

  claim_types          ≥ 0.95   (observed 100%)
  deduplication        ≥ 0.95   (observed 100%)
  contested_detection  ≥ 0.95   (observed 100%)
  confidence_scoring   ≥ 0.90   (observed 100%)
  real_world           ≥ 0.85   (observed ~92%, P=84.5% R=100%)
  robustness           ≥ 0.45   (observed ~53%; adversarial set, lower bar)
  edge_cases           ≥ 0.95   (observed ~99%)

Categories not in the map are skipped so newly-added suites don't
fail CI before a baseline lands. Updates to a floor (intentional
regression or improvement) bump the value in the same commit so the
expected baseline stays auditable.

Already runs on every PR via the existing go test ./data/eval/... in
.github/workflows/ci.yml — no new workflow file needed.

LongMemEval / LoCoMo per-suite gates are gated on the
honest-competitor-benchmarks task landing the adapters; relationship-
detection eval at internal/relate uses binary case tests, a different
gate shape outside this task's spec.

Closes roady task-eval-ci-regression-gate.

commits.subject: feat(security): scope filter on trust path, clearer JWT rotation prompt
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(security): scope filter on trust path, clearer JWT rotation prompt

The audit flagged that mcpRunWhichTestToTrust and `mnemos trust`
enumerate test_result claims across every run/service/team — a low-
privilege caller can list tests outside the contexts it should see.

Add a per-call scope filter:
  - mcpWhichTestToTrustInput: optional service/env/team fields, all
    omitempty so existing callers with no filter return everything as
    before.
  - cmd/mnemos/trust.go: --service / --env / --team flags route into
    a domain.Scope filter applied before scoring.
  - In both paths, candidates are narrowed via Scope.Matches before
    BuildReport runs.

Test TestMcpRunWhichTestToTrust_ScopeFilter seeds a 2-env candidate
set (billing/prod + billing/staging) and asserts env=prod returns
exactly the prod claim.

Separately, the auto-generated-JWT-secret message at first boot was a
single line; rewrite as a multi-line operator banner explaining the
implications (prior tokens invalidated), backup recommendation, the
preferred MNEMOS_JWT_SECRET injection path for production, and the
90-day rotation cadence with MNEMOS_JWT_PREV_SECRET overlap.

JWT kid header + key-id-driven verification (the deeper fix flagged
in the same audit row) is split into its own roady task — that work
is structural to internal/auth/jwt.go and earned independent scoping.

Closes roady task-jwt-kid-rotation-scope-filter (scope half).

commits.subject: feat(observability): RED metrics + SLO doc + parity matrix entry
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(observability): RED metrics + SLO doc + parity matrix entry

Step 2 of the observability bundle. Operators couldn't answer "is
mnemos healthy right now?" beyond /health — no Prometheus surface,
no SLO target, no error budget.

cmd/mnemos/serve_metrics.go:
  - Private prometheus registry (avoids the global-singleton trap of
    re-registration panics in tests).
  - mnemos_http_requests_total{method,route,status} counter.
  - mnemos_http_request_duration_seconds{method,route,status}
    histogram with buckets tight at the SLO threshold (5ms..10s).
  - mnemos_http_in_flight_requests gauge.
  - metricsMiddleware sits inside boltAccessLog so prometheus duration
    matches the logged duration. Route label comes from the
    *registered* mux pattern (http.ServeMux.Handler) — avoids the
    cardinality explosion of using the raw URL.
  - /internal/metrics endpoint via promhttp.HandlerFor with
    EnableOpenMetrics for exemplar support.

SLO.md: 99.9% availability over 30d (43-min budget), p99 read 250ms,
p99 write 500ms, error rate ceilings, PromQL examples for each, and a
budget-burn policy that escalates from "pause feature work" at 25%
remaining to "freeze deploys" at 5%.

Step 1 (X-Request-ID middleware) shipped in 21fb5fb.
Step 3 (SBOM/cosign/SLSA/arm64 docker) is pure release pipeline work
and split into its own roady task.

API parity matrix gains a row for /internal/metrics (HTTP-only by
design, gRPC has its own server-reflection plus the existing Metrics
RPC).

Tests TestServe_MetricsEndpointExposesPrometheusFormat.

Closes roady task-request-id-red-metrics-slo (steps 1+2 of 3).

commits.subject: feat(observability): add X-Request-ID middleware and access-log field
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(observability): add X-Request-ID middleware and access-log field

Step 1 of the request-id + RED + SLO + supply-chain bundle. Without
correlation ids across HTTP / MCP / pipeline boundaries, tracing one
LLM-grounded query through extraction → embedding → SQL → response
required guessing from timestamps. Hard at the best of times,
impossible under load.

requestIDMiddleware decorates every request with X-Request-ID:

  - Accepts inbound X-Request-ID so distributed tracers (OTel, Datadog,
    Jaeger) can pin a single id end-to-end. Allowed charset alnum +
    `-_.` capped at 64 chars; anything else (spaces, control chars,
    over-long ids) is discarded and a fresh id is minted to defend
    against header-injection abuse.
  - Mints 16 bytes of crypto/rand as 32 lowercase hex when no header
    is present.
  - Wall-clock fallback if rand.Read fails so the access log never
    drops the field.
  - Stores the id in context (withRequestID / requestIDFromContext)
    so downstream handlers and the bolt access logger can reference
    it without re-parsing the header.

The middleware sits inside securityHeaders so the X-Request-ID
response header is always set, and ahead of boltAccessLog so the
access log line carries `request_id` as its first field.

Tests TestRequestID_GeneratedWhenAbsent, _PassedThroughWhenSupplied,
_RejectsHostileInputAndMintsFresh.

Steps 2 (Prometheus RED metrics on /internal/metrics + SLO.md) and
3 (SBOMs / cosign / SLSA / arm64 docker manifests) remain pending in
the same roady task.

commits.subject: feat(trust): operator-readable prose rationale alongside metric string
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(trust): operator-readable prose rationale alongside metric string

The trust scorer's compact rationale string assumed the reader knew
what citations=3(0.42) means and what 0.5 default authority signals —
fine for tooling, opaque to non-technical operators using
which_test_to_trust to pick a winner.

Add a second, prose rationale alongside the metric string. Single
source of truth: trust.BuildReport now returns
(score, signals, rationale, prose). buildProseRationale generates
plain-English sentences for each provenance signal:
  - "Last ran today (fresh)." / "Last ran 30 days ago (stale)."
  - "Passed 9 of 10 runs (decisive)." / "(flaky)." / "(mixed)."
  - "Live test." / "Stale source." / "Dead source."
  - "High-authority source (0.85)." / "Authority not configured (assumed neutral)."
  - "Corroborated by 3 citations." (only when count > 0)
  - "Submitting agent has reduced authority (0.40)."

Surface paths:
  - domain.ProvenanceReport gains ProseRationale field
  - query.WhyTrustClaim populates both rationale + prose
  - cmd/mnemos/trust.go emits prose_rationale per candidate + winner
  - cmd/mnemos/mcp.go which_test_to_trust mirrors
  - trust.ScoreWithProse convenience helper for callers that only
    want score+prose without the structured signals

Tests TestBuildReport_ProseRationale_TestSignals cover three shapes
(fresh decisive, stale flaky, minimum). Existing parity test extended
to assert prose is non-empty for every BuildReport call.

Doc updates:
  - CLAUDE.md: prompt version v1.2 → v1.4 (matches prompt.go),
    document MNEMOS_LLM_CACHE_MAX_BYTES.
  - README.md: hybrid BM25 + cosine retrieval note at line 97 so
    new users don't underestimate retrieval quality.

Closes roady task-operator-readable-trust-rationale.

commits.subject: perf(infra): pool tuning for pg/mysql, LLM cache eviction, doc env vars
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
perf(infra): pool tuning for pg/mysql, LLM cache eviction, doc env vars

Two unrelated infra hardening fixes plus one doc update.

1. Postgres/MySQL pool sizing. Both providers called sql.Open + Ping
   and never tuned the pool — Go's database/sql defaults to unlimited
   open connections, which exhausts Postgres max_connections (100)
   under MCP burst load and silently leaks idle conns through load
   balancers that recycle TCP sessions. Add tuneConnPool to each
   provider:
     - MaxOpenConns: 25 (default; env MNEMOS_DB_MAX_CONNS)
     - MaxIdleConns: 5  (default; env MNEMOS_DB_MAX_IDLE_CONNS)
     - ConnMaxLifetime: 30m (default; env MNEMOS_DB_CONN_MAX_LIFETIME)
   Negative or unparseable env values fall back to default.

2. LLM extraction cache eviction. data/cache/llm-extraction/ grew
   unbounded — long-running ingestion or shared CI runners filled
   disk silently. evictCacheIfOverCap runs on every storeCachedClaims
   write: lists dir, sums sizes, sorts oldest-first by mtime, removes
   until under cap. Default 1 GiB; MNEMOS_LLM_CACHE_MAX_BYTES
   overrides (0 disables — opt-in for benchmark runs that want every
   cache hit).

3. CLAUDE.md env section: document the four new vars.

Tests: TestEvictCacheIfOverCap_RemovesOldestUntilUnderCap,
TestEvictCacheIfOverCap_NoopUnderCap,
TestEvictCacheIfOverCap_DisabledWhenCapZero,
TestLLMCacheMaxBytes_EnvOverride.

Closes roady task-postgres-mysql-connection-pool-tuning + LLM cache
eviction.

commits.subject: test(api): add cross-transport parity fitness function
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
test(api): add cross-transport parity fitness function

Mnemos exposes the same capabilities through three independent
transports (MCP, HTTP, gRPC) — they were written separately and have
silently drifted. which_test_to_trust shipped only on MCP; Metrics
existed only on gRPC. Drift is fine when intentional; the failure
mode is silent drift.

Add cmd/mnemos/api_parity_test.go: enumerates each surface by string
name (regex over srve.Tool / mux.HandleFunc / func (s *Server) X(ctx
context.Context...)) and cross-references against a parityMatrix
declaration. The matrix records every capability across the three
columns; parityNA marks deliberate asymmetries (e.g. /v1/leads is
HTTP-only by design). The test fails with a named drift list when a
new tool/route/method ships without a matrix entry — forcing the
author to think about cross-transport coverage before merging.

The test caught one previously-undocumented gRPC method (Metrics) on
first run; added to the matrix.

Step 1 of the feature spec (fitness function) is in place; the full
internal/app/ use-case layer that would let HTTP/gRPC/MCP become
20-line transcoders remains a separate, larger task.

Closes roady task-api-surface-parity-fitness-function.

commits.subject: feat(security): harden POST /v1/leads with rate limit, validation, logging
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(security): harden POST /v1/leads with rate limit, validation, logging

The public POST /v1/leads endpoint bypassed JWT auth, accepted any
string containing "@" as an email, and used fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
"lead captured email=%s\n", req.Email) — three independent failure
modes shipping in one handler:

  - DoS: no rate limiter let any caller flood the process and the
    log volume.
  - Log injection: a CRLF in req.Email would forge log lines.
  - PII: plaintext addresses landed in stderr indefinitely (GDPR).

Hardening:

  - validateLeadEmail enforces 254-byte cap (RFC 5321), strict
    syntax via net/mail.ParseAddress, rejection of any control
    character (CR/LF/null/DEL) anywhere in the trimmed input,
    lowercased normalisation. Trailing whitespace is still trimmed
    so legitimate copy-paste typos don't bounce.
  - serve_ratelimit.go adds a per-IP token-bucket via
    golang.org/x/time/rate (5 req/min, burst 10) with a lazy 30-min
    TTL sweep so one-off submitters don't pin a limiter forever.
    Throttled responses set Retry-After: 60.
  - makeLeadsHandler writes through a bolt structured logger; the
    log line carries email_sha256 (not the address) and remote_ip,
    so JSON-escaped fields close the log-injection vector and the
    log file is no longer a plaintext PII trail.
  - Body decode switched from io.LimitReader to http.MaxBytesReader
    so slowloris streams can't keep the handler alive past 1 MiB.
  - clientIP honours X-Forwarded-For (leftmost) when present.

go.mod: + golang.org/x/time v0.15.0

Tests TestValidateLeadEmail (13 cases), TestLeadsRateLimit_Throttles,
TestLeadsHandler_RejectsInvalidEmail (incl. CRLF/unknown field),
TestLeadsHandler_RejectsNonPost cover the contract.

Closes roady task-public-/v1/leads-hardening.

commits.subject: feat(security): add securityHeaders middleware (CSP, HSTS, frame, etc)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(security): add securityHeaders middleware (CSP, HSTS, frame, etc)

The HTTP server emitted no security headers — landing page and
registry SPA were exposed to clickjacking, XSS escalation, MIME
sniffing, and full Referer leakage to any external link clicked from
within. Add securityHeaders middleware between panicRecover and
boltAccessLog so every response (including handler panics and the
auth-bypassed /v1/leads endpoint) ships:

  - Content-Security-Policy: same-origin scripts/styles/forms,
    frame-ancestors 'none', base-uri 'self', object-src 'none'.
    'unsafe-inline' kept on script-src as a transition concession
    for the landing page's inline submit handler — tighten when the
    inline script is moved to a static asset.
  - X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
  - X-Frame-Options: DENY
  - Referrer-Policy: no-referrer
  - X-XSS-Protection: 0  (disable legacy auditor; modern browsers ignore)
  - Permissions-Policy: deny camera/microphone/geolocation/payment
  - Strict-Transport-Security: 2-year preload-list-eligible, but ONLY
    when MNEMOS_TLS_CERT_FILE + MNEMOS_TLS_KEY_FILE are set.
    Plaintext HTTP local-dev requests would otherwise brick on
    subsequent loads.

Tests TestServe_SecurityHeadersOnPublicRoutes and
TestServe_HSTSOnlyWithTLSEnv assert the contract on /, /app, /health.

Closes roady task-security-headers-middleware.

commits.subject: test(trust): cover mcpRunWhichTestToTrust verdict paths
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
test(trust): cover mcpRunWhichTestToTrust verdict paths

Newly-shipped epistemic-provenance surface in cmd/mnemos/mcp.go was
unverified — verdict thresholds, requirement_ref validation, the
empty-candidate path, and the test_result-only filter all shipped
without tests. Add cmd/mnemos/trust_test.go with five table-driven
cases:

  - PicksFreshHigherPassRatio: 30-day-stale 5/5 vs 2-hour-old 10/0
    must surface the fresh decisive run as the winner.
  - AmbiguousVerdictWhenMarginTight: two identical candidates produce
    score gap < 0.05, so the output verdict reads "ambiguous" rather
    than silently picking one.
  - NoCandidates: requirement_ref with no matching test_result claims
    returns the no_candidates verdict and an empty candidates slice.
  - RequiresRequirementRef: empty input string is rejected with an
    error mentioning the field name.
  - FilterScopedToTestResultOnly: the underlying
    ListByTestRequirementRef filter must enforce
    type='test_result' even when other claim types share text that
    references the requirement.

Tests use openConn against a per-test sqlite temp path via
t.Setenv("MNEMOS_DB_URL", ...), exercising the same backend the real
MCP handler hits.

Closes roady task-test-coverage-for-mnemos-trust-cli.

commits.subject: feat(trust): ListByTestRequirementRef port + index for which-test-to-trust
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(trust): ListByTestRequirementRef port + index for which-test-to-trust

cmd/mnemos/trust.go and the which_test_to_trust MCP tool used to call
Claims.ListAll(ctx) and filter by TestRequirementRef in Go — O(n) over
the entire claim corpus per invocation, with all 30+ columns marshaled
over the wire on Postgres/MySQL.

Add ListByTestRequirementRef(ctx, ref) ([]Claim, error) to
ports.ClaimRepository. Implementations:
  - sqlite: sqlc query ListClaimsByTestRequirementRef + partial index
    idx_claims_test_requirement_ref ON claims(test_requirement_ref)
    WHERE test_requirement_ref != ''
  - memory: filter the ordered claim slice and sort TestLastRunAt DESC
    then CreatedAt DESC to mirror SQL ordering
  - postgres / mysql: parameterised SELECT against the same partial
    index pattern (note: scanClaimRow on those backends does not yet
    hydrate test_provenance fields — pre-existing gap, tracked
    separately)
  - libsql: reuses the sqlite repository unchanged

cmd/mnemos/trust.go and cmd/mnemos/mcp.go now call the port directly;
the ListAll-then-filter loops are gone.

Side cleanup:
  - Drop duplicate `idx_events_run_id` declaration in sql/sqlite/schema.sql.
  - Restore decisions.sql parity with the schema's refuted_beliefs_json
    and failed_outcome_id columns. The hand-edited sqlcgen had drifted
    from the SQL queries; running `make sqlc` previously broke
    DecisionRepository because the generator regenerated to match the
    queries (which were missing those fields).
  - Narrow em-dash to ASCII colon in the new SQL comment. sqlc 1.30.0
    has a byte-vs-rune offset bug that corrupted every query in
    claims.sql.go when the file contained a multi-byte UTF-8 character.

Full test suite green across all five backends.

Closes roady task-claimrepository.listbytestrequirementref.

commits.subject: refactor(trust): collapse duplicate signal calculation into BuildReport
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
refactor(trust): collapse duplicate signal calculation into BuildReport

The trust weight constants (0.50/0.15/0.13/0.10/0.05/0.07) and per-
signal calculations were duplicated across internal/trust/credibility.go
and internal/query/engine.go provenanceSignals — kept in sync only by a
"must stay in sync" comment. A fitness-function smell: nothing failed
when they drifted.

Add trust.BuildReport(in) (score, []ProvenanceSignal, rationale) as the
canonical implementation. ScoreCredibility becomes a thin wrapper. Query
engine's WhyTrustClaim consumes BuildReport directly; the ~140 lines of
duplicated provenanceSignals + recencyDetail + clamp01 + maxInt +
livenessWeight in engine.go are gone.

Invariant guards: TestBuildReport_ScoreCredibilityParity asserts the
wrapper produces the same score and rationale as BuildReport across 5
varied inputs (test claims, agent-authority, low-trust, high-trust,
flaky tests). TestBuildReport_SignalContributionsSumToScore asserts the
additive signal contributions sum to the returned score within float
tolerance — catches any future drift even if the parity test passes.

Closes roady task-deduplicate-trust-signal-calculation.

commits.subject: fix(landing): escape apostrophe in lead-form alert
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
fix(landing): escape apostrophe in lead-form alert

Unescaped apostrophe in alert('Thank you! We'll be in touch soon.')
closed the string literal early, throwing SyntaxError at script parse
time. The whole <script> block failed to parse, so the form submit
handler never bound, the mailto fallback never fired, and CTA click
tracking was dead. Switching the outer quotes to double-quotes
restores the entire script.

commits.subject: chore: sync roady + relicta artifacts
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
chore: sync roady + relicta artifacts

Roady state reflects the epistemic-provenance task transitioning
through start/complete; relicta release pointer advances to the run
captured during this session.

commits.subject: feat(trust): test-aware epistemic provenance and conflict resolution
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(trust): test-aware epistemic provenance and conflict resolution

Closes the epistemic-provenance task. Test-aware credibility: when a
claim is a test_result, ScoreCredibility prefers TestLastRunAt over
claim-row recency and adds a test_decisiveness signal (|pass-fail|/
total) so 50/50 flaky tests deflate while 10/0 stable tests boost.
Test-aware contradiction resolver: pickTestWinner runs before the
generic confidence/trust path — for two test_result claims under the
same TestRequirementRef, the more recent run (≥24h gap) or higher
pass-ratio (≥0.2 gap) wins with explicit rationale, falling back to
generic resolution otherwise. New CLI `mnemos trust --test=<ref>` and
MCP tool `which_test_to_trust` rank candidates and surface the winner
plus runners-up so an agent can answer "which test should I trust?"
without reading evidence row by row. WhyTrustClaim and the per-claim
ScoreCredibility pass populate the new test fields, and provenance
signals expose a test_decisiveness entry for test claims.

commits.subject: feat(landing): serve marketing landing at / and split registry to /app
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(landing): serve marketing landing at / and split registry to /app

Mounts landing.html at GET / and moves the registry SPA to GET /app so
visitors land on positioning copy rather than the JSON dashboard. Adds
a public POST /v1/leads opt-out from JWT auth so the landing form can
capture interest without an account. serve_test split into landing,
registry-app, and unknown-path coverage.

commits.raw_message: docs(landing): sharpen category framing and differentiation messaging
